name: Collado de Huerta Grande – circular from Cómpeta - Parque Natural Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama
name_original: Collado de Huerta Grande – circular desde Cómpeta - Parque Natural Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama
region_intro: Hiking around Cómpeta offers access to the Sierra Almijara mountains, a region characterized by diverse terrain within the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park. The landscape features rugged mountain areas, shaded pine forests, extensive olive groves, and terraced vineyards. Situated at an altitude of 638 meters, the village provides immediate access to hilly and mountainous trails, with views extending to the Mediterranean Sea. The area also includes rivers, streams, and ancient irrigation channels, providing varied walking paths. Best hiking trails around Cómpeta The most popular hiking route is Collado de Huerta Grande – circular from Cómpeta - Parque Natural Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama, a difficult 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain within the natural park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cómpeta – Canillas de Albaida loop from Cómpeta, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.1 km) path. This trail connects two historic villages, showcasing the region's landscapes. Local hikers also love the Cómpeta – View of Cómpeta loop from Cómpeta, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail leading through hilly terrain with panoramic viewpoints, often completed in about 2 hours 37 minutes. Hiking around Cómpeta is defined by mountainous terrain, pine forests, and terraced vineyards. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely walks to challenging climbs. The routes in Cómpeta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 920 reviews. More than 3,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Cómpeta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cómpeta?
A: Cómpeta offers a wide variety of hiking experiences with over 60 trails. These routes cater to different fitness levels, ranging from leisurely strolls to challenging climbs through the mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families in Cómpeta?
A: Yes, Cómpeta provides 16 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or families. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38271639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hawk Hill – Huerta Grande Pass loop from Cómpeta&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.4 km trail that offers pleasant views without significant elevation gain.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking near Cómpeta?
A: Hiking around Cómpeta offers diverse landscapes, from the rugged mountains and pine forests of the Sierra Almijara Natural Park to terraced vineyards and olive groves. You'll also encounter serene riverside paths and ancient irrigation channels, with many routes providing panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and, on clear days, the distant Sierra Nevada.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Cómpeta?
A: Yes, many routes around Cómpeta are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1284946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cómpeta – Canillas de Albaida loop from Cómpeta&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.1 km path connecting two historic villages, showcasing the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What are some notable attractions or points of interest I can see along the hiking trails?
A: The region around Cómpeta is rich in natural and historical attractions. You can explore fascinating geological formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/325807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nerja Cave&lt;/a&gt;, or discover various mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/283276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huerta Grande Pass&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning viewpoints. Some trails also lead to historic villages like El Acebuchal.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cómpeta?
A: Cómpeta's climate, with over 320 days of sunshine annually, makes it suitable for hiking almost year-round. However, spring and autumn offer particularly ideal conditions with pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winters are generally mild.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to natural water features or potential swimming spots?
A: Yes, the region features serene riverside routes and ancient irrigation channels. Some trails, like those leading to the 'Lost Village' of El Acebuchal, offer opportunities to paddle in the river. The Caminito del Rey de la Axarquía, a nearby trail, is also known for its natural swimming spots and waterfalls.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Cómpeta?
A: The hiking trails in Cómpeta are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 950 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, the breathtaking panoramic views of the mountains and the Mediterranean, and the well-maintained paths that lead through varied ecosystems like pine forests and olive groves.

Q: Are there any shaded routes, especially for hiking during warmer months?
A: Yes, some routes offer significant shade, which is particularly welcome during warmer periods. The popular trail leading to the 'Lost Village' of El Acebuchal is largely shaded, providing a cooler hiking experience through its natural surroundings.

Q: Can I find trails that connect different villages in the area?
A: Absolutely. The network of trails around Cómpeta often connects the picturesque white villages of the region. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1284946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cómpeta – Canillas de Albaida loop from Cómpeta&lt;/a&gt; is a scenic journey that links these two beautiful historic villages, allowing you to experience the local culture and landscapes.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced hikers looking for a strenuous climb?
A: For experienced hikers, Cómpeta offers several challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995588500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Huerta Grande – circular from Cómpeta&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 8.7 km trail with significant elevation gain, providing a rewarding experience within the natural park. The El Lucero Peak Trail also leads to a summit over 1,770 meters, offering unmatched panoramic views.
tips: This impressive typical Andalusian white village is surrounded by the mountains of the Sierra de Almijara and is famous for its sweet Moscatel wine.
Nice rest area with refuge and great views in good weather
Nice view on different sides. A few meters up the hill to the locked fire department and you have a wonderful all-round view, including Cerro Lucero, Cerro Verde, etc.
This viewpoint is located just before reaching the town of Cómpeta. On clear days you can see the coast of Nerja and Torrox.
